communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

In 2024, NOAA launched an investigation in response to this complaint being filed to the agency. The complaint alleged that Kennedy illegally used a chainsaw to decapitate a dead whale in the 1990s in Hyannis Point, MA before transporting it to his NY home. As evidence, the complaint cited a 2012 Town and Country profile of Kick Kennedy, RFK Jr.'s daughter, who tells an anecdote describing such an incident: https://www.townandcountrymag.com/society/tradition/a924/kick-kennedy-interview/ Kick Kennedy was born in 1988, and she describes this incident happening when she was approximately 6 years old.

If this incident indeed occurred, it's conceivable that another resident of Hyannis Port later found the remains of a whale with a head or large limb missing and noticed wounds that aren't consistent with entanglement or a vessel strike. I'd imagine that this person would call the police.

Therefore, accordingly, pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records (dated between January 1, 1994 and December 31, 1996):
(1) All CAD repots that mention a "whale" or "whales" — and if any of them led to the Barnstable creating an incident reports, I would like all copies of said incident reports.

I respectfully request that my request is expedited, given RFK Jr.'s current role at the Department of Health and Human Services. If this incident occurred, it would also be in violation of federal law under the Marine Mammal Protection Act, which forbids transporting protected animal remains across state lines. I imagine any records produced wouldn't definitively prove RFK Jr. did this, but since NOAA closed its investigation before contacting any other agencies about relevant records,  it's within the public interest for a journalist to investigate this incident.

I am a news reporter for WIRED (wired.com), so the requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. Therefore, I request that any fees are waived. But in the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available.

If you need to contact me, you can reach me at caroline_haskins@wired.com, or here. Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,
Caroline Haskins
